# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains preliminary empirical evidence about AI configuration file adoption in open-source software projects, the kinds of information and writing styles used in AGENTS.md files, and how AGENTS.md files change over time.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study investigated 466 open-source software projects for adoption and analyzed how AI configuration files are structured and evolve.

## Source Excerpt

“we present the results of a preliminary study investigating the adoption of AI configuration files in 466 open-source software projects, what information developers provide in AGENTS.md files, how they present that information, and how the files evolve over time.”

## Note

This statement is from the abstract and frames the study scope as preliminary.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: result
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The authors report that there is not yet an established structure for organizing content in AGENTS.md files.

## Source Excerpt

“Our findings indicate that there is no established structure yet for organizing content in AGENTS.md files”

## Note

This is a direct reported finding from a preliminary study, so the lack of established structure should be treated as current observed variation rather than a permanent state.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: result
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors report that AGENTS.md files vary in how context is presented, including descriptive, prescriptive, prohibitive, explanatory, and conditional styles.

## Source Excerpt

“there is a lot of variation in terms of how context is provided (descriptive, prescriptive, prohibitive, explanatory, conditional).”

## Note

These style labels are given explicitly in the abstract.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The preliminary study’s RQ1 results were based on data collected from 10,000 GitHub repositories.

## Source Excerpt

“The results of our preliminary study are based on data collected from 10,000 GitHub repositories ( RQ1 ).”

## Note

This evidence applies specifically to the adoption analysis.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The final repository sample before selecting the 10,000-study subset contained 49,311 repositories.

## Source Excerpt

“This resulted in a final sample of 49,311 repositories.”

## Note

The paper describes this as the broader pool from which the 10,000 repositories for the work-in-progress paper were selected.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Only 466 repositories, or 5% of the repositories scanned, had adopted at least one of the studied AI configuration file formats.

## Source Excerpt

“Only 466 (5%) of the repositories that we scanned had already adopted at least one of the formats that we considered.”

## Note

The paper immediately qualifies this by noting that only four commercial tools were included, so the measured adoption rate does not cover all agent tooling.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Among repositories with AI configuration files, TypeScript was the most common main language with 135 repositories.

## Source Excerpt

“We found AI configuration files in 135 repositories with TypeScript as main language, 58 Go, 58 Python, 56 C#, 36 Java, 34 JavaScript, 32 C++, 29 Rust, 19 PHP, and 9 C.”

## Note

This is a distribution count within the studied sample, not a normalized adoption rate by language.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Copilot instruction files were the longest on average at 310 lines with a standard deviation of 127 lines.

## Source Excerpt

“The Copilot instruction files were on average the longest ( M = 310 M=310 lines, S ​ D = 127 SD=127 lines)”

## Note

The paper compares file lengths across four configuration file formats.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
AGENTS.md files had the highest file-length variation, with a mean of 142 lines and a standard deviation of 231 lines.

## Source Excerpt

“AGENTS.md files had the highest variation ( M = 142 M=142 , S ​ D = 231 SD=231 ).”

## Note

The high standard deviation indicates substantial variation in AGENTS.md file length within the sample.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The RQ2 heading analysis excluded 15 AGENTS.md files created before January 1, 2025 and excluded 5 AGENTS.md files with no heading structure.

## Source Excerpt

“We excluded 15 AGENTS.md files that were created before January 1, 2025, i.e., before the AGENTS.md convention was introduced.”
“Five repositories contained AGENTS.md files without any heading structure; these were excluded from the analysis.”

## Note

These exclusions narrow the analyzed subset for the section-heading structure results.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Conventions/best practices was the most common AGENTS.md heading category in the table, with 50 level-1 or level-2 headings.

## Source Excerpt

“Conventions/best practices ... 50”

## Note

This count is reported in Table 1 as number of level 1 or 2 headings.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors identified five stylistic dimensions in AGENTS.md convention sections: descriptive, prescriptive, prohibitive, explanatory, and conditional.

## Source Excerpt

“We found that the writing style can be characterized along five stylistic dimensions: descriptive , prescriptive , prohibitive , explanatory , and conditional .”

## Note

This result comes from analysis of 50 sections labeled Conventions/Best Practices.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Section 4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Among 155 AGENTS.md files, 77 files (50%) had not changed after the initial commit, 36 files (23%) changed once, and 32 files (21%) changed between two and seven times.

## Source Excerpt

“To answer RQ3 , we first screened the commit histories of all 155 AGENTS.md files and found that 77 (50%) of them had not been changed after the initial commit, 36 (23%) only once, and 32 (21%) between two and seven times.”

## Note

These counts describe commit-history distribution for the 155 AGENTS.md files screened, not the full repository sample.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In AGENTS.md files with at least 10 commits, the most frequent change categories were Add instruction(s) with 78 instances and Modify instruction(s) with 59 instances.

## Source Excerpt

“Table 2 shows that the most frequent change categories are ‘ Add instruction(s) ’ and ‘ Modify instruction(s) ’.”
“Add instruction(s) ... 78”
“Modify instruction(s) ... 59”

## Note

These frequencies are from the annotated subset of files with at least 10 commits, not all AGENTS.md files.
---
